exemplar.title: Investigating the effect of mass and radius on the angular velocity of a moving cartwheel

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating the effect of mass and radius on the angular velocity of a moving cartwheel

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data tables are well labeled with units, averages and processed values.
Graphs are generally clear and correctly plotted.
Data processing (averages, conversion from period to angular velocity, graphical fits) is executed without major numerical errors.
weaknesses: Instrument uncertainties and propagation to derived quantities (e.g., Ï) are omitted.
Error bars are incomplete or missing, and outlier treatment is not addressed.
Minor inconsistencies (misspelled headings, terminology) and lack of regression analysis limit precision.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data Analysis sits clearly in the 3â4 band: communication of recording is either clear or precise, and calculations are accurate. However, significant omissions in uncertainty handling and graph completeness prevent a 5.

strengths: Methodology is described in a clear, step-by-step format enabling reproducibility.
Annotated sketches, apparatus tables and control-variable justifications are thorough.
Procedures (gun alignment, mass increments, camera placement, trial numbers) are detailed and well justified.
weaknesses: Definition of key terms (e.g., âoscillationâ) is ambiguous.
Launch force or muzzle velocity is not quantified, limiting insight into initial conditions.
Calibration details (e.g., balance calibration date) are omitted, and background context remains broader than ideal.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Research Design demonstrates consistently strong performance across methodological strands. Two strands comfortably sit in the 5â6 band with detailed reproducibility; only minor context and quantification ambiguities prevent perfection but still justify the top score of 6 given the overall rigor and clarity.

strengths: Conclusion is consistent with data trends and supported by reference to graphs and calculated values.
Relevant scientific concepts (moment of inertia, torque, angular momentum) are invoked.
Comparison to accepted scientific context (e.g., external studies) is present and relevant.
weaknesses: Lacks quantitative justification (no fit parameters or percentage changes).
No numerical benchmarking against published data or explicit numerical comparison.
Justification is qualitative rather than fully supported by analysis outcomes.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Conclusion meets the descriptor for 3â4: it is described and supported by analysis but not fully quantified or rigorously benchmarked, placing it solidly in the middle of the band.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified (e.g., uneven projectile impact, friction).
Realistic and relevant improvements are proposed (fixed mounts, material changes, smoother surfaces).
weaknesses: Does not explain the relative impact or prioritize which limitation dominates.
Improvements, while relevant, lack detailed explanation of expected precision gains.
No quantitative estimation of how changes would reduce uncertainties.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation demonstrates clear identification of specific weaknesses and realistic improvements (3â4 band). Explanations remain concise without full development of relative impacts, preventing a higher mark.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student formulates a clear research question and provides some background on human cartwheels versus a rigidâbody model.  Although the rationale is relevant, the context remains fairly broad (general rotational mechanics and gymnastics) and lacks a tight link to a specific real-world or scholarly context; hence it does not fully meet the descriptor for the highest band.

Random uncertainties for time are calculated and fractional/percentage uncertainties tabulated; limited error bars appear on graphs.  However, instrument uncertainties are not stated, propagation to Ï is omitted and some error bars are incomplete, leaving significant omissions.  This fits mid-band (3-4) but nearer its lower value.

Methodological considerations (control variables, justification of increments, constant release force, use of tracking software) are explained and explicitly linked to collecting sufficient and relevant data to address the research question.  Explanations show insight, although a quantitative estimate of required precision is missing, warranting 5 rather than 6.

Specific weaknesses (uneven projectile impact, mass variation between wheels, friction/air resistance) are described and their effects briefly outlined, giving more depth than a generic list but not fully explaining relative magnitude or quantitative impact, aligning with 3-4 marks.

Step-by-step instructions, annotated sketches, apparatus tables and control-variable justifications allow another investigator to reproduce the work with only minor interpretation.  Details such as Nerf-gun alignment, mass increments, camera placement and trial numbers are adequate, so the strand sits comfortably in the 5-6 band, albeit with a couple of small ambiguities (e.g., definition of âoscillationâ).

Averages, conversion from period to angular velocity and graphical fits are executed correctly; no major numerical mistakes are evident.  Nonetheless, propagation of uncertainty, regression analysis and residual checks are missing, so processing is accurate but not fully comprehensiveâappropriate for the upper end of 3-4.

Improvements such as fixed gun mounts, lighter materials and smoother surfaces are realistic and relevant; some rationale is provided, yet explanations remain concise rather than fully developed or prioritised, meeting descriptor for 3-4 and deserving the higher score within that band.

Data tables are labelled with units, averages and processed values; graphs are present and generally readable.  Minor inconsistencies (mis-spelt headings, âoscillationsâ terminology) prevent describing communication as simultaneously crystal-clear and fully precise, but it is at least clear OR precise, justifying the 3-4 band; quality leans toward the top of that band.

The student compares findings to accepted conceptsâmoment of inertia, torque, angular momentumâand cites external studies/graphs.  The comparison is relevant and partially developed but not rigorously analysed or numerically benchmarked, matching the middle band.

The conclusion (Ï decreases with mass and radius) is consistent with the presented data and supported by reference to graphs and calculated values.  While justification is provided through qualitative linkage to rotational dynamics, quantitative reasoning is limited, so the strand achieves the 3-4 band rather than the top.

annotations.comment: The research question is clearly stated but framed within a broad context. Narrow the focus by linking it to a specific real-world or scholarly scenario (e.g., particular gymnastics applications or published studies).
Background section describes human cartwheels well but lacks citations to specific literature. Incorporate peer-reviewed biomechanical studies to substantiate the phases and energy considerations.

The explanation âforce, F, is inversely proportional to radius, râ misconstrues the torque equation $$\tau=Fr\sin\theta$$. Clarify that to maintain constant torque, F must vary as 1/r, not that Fâ1/r inherently.

Hypothesis draws on two conflicting formulas for angular velocity without reconciling them. Ensure theoretical derivation is consistent by choosing the correct expression and justifying why mass or radius should appear.
Dependent variable symbol t is overloaded (total time vs period). Adopt distinct symbols (e.g., $$t$$ for total time, $$T$$ for period, $$\omega$$ for angular velocity) and define them at first use.
Control-variable table is thorough, detailing methods and justifications. This level of detail supports reproducibility.
Apparatus list includes tolerances but omits calibration details (e.g., balance calibration date). Add calibration info to strengthen methodological rigor.
Preliminary test section states outcome without presenting data or observations. Include sample timing or images to justify final setup choices.
The Nerf-gun mechanism is justified but lacks quantification of launch force or muzzle velocity. Measuring and reporting the bullet speed would allow calculation of initial angular momentum.
Procedures are detailed and sequential, enabling reproducibility. The step-by-step format is a notable strength.
Camera setup is controlled but lacks specification of frame rate and resolution. Report these to ensure data-recording precision.
Uncertainty calculation uses range/2 but omits instrument resolution uncertainty. Incorporate scale & stopwatch resolution into $$\Delta t$$ for completeness.
Uncertainty values vary unexpectedly (e.g., 0.216 s > 0.134 s). Check maxâmin ranges for transcription errors and explain anomalies.
Raw data for radius includes an outlier trial (T1 = 2.934 s at r = 7 cm). Identify and justify treatment of outliers (e.g., repeat trial or statistical exclusion).
Processed angular velocities lack propagated uncertainties. Apply error propagation to $$\omega=2\pi/T$$ to assign error bars.
Graph 1 (Ï vs r) is clear but omits error bars and model fit equation. Add uncertainties and a regression line to quantify the relationship.
Graph 2 (Ï vs m) lacks axis labels with units, making interpretation difficult. Include âMass (g)â and âAngular velocity (rad sâ»Â¹)â labels.
Discussion uses inconsistent formulas for moment of inertia (sums vs Â½MRÂ²). Clarify the correct expression for combined discs and sticks by writing total $$I=\sum mr^2$$ or appropriate geometry.
Safety section overlooks potential ricochet of Nerf bullets. Include risk assessment for projectile bounce or accidental hits.
Conclusion restates trends qualitatively but omits quantitative comparison (e.g., fit parameters or percentage decreases). Include numerical justification linking data to hypothesis.
Comparison to Fish & Ostwald is noted, but lacks numerical benchmarking. Discuss how your slope or curvature matches or diverges from published data.
Future research section outlines intriguing extensions but lacks defined research questions and success metrics. Formulate clear objectives and measurable outcomes.
Strengths section claims error bars were used, yet earlier graphs omit them. Ensure consistency by including the mentioned error bars or revising the claim.
Limitations list specific sources of error but does not rank their relative impact. Evaluate which factor (e.g., friction vs mass variation) dominates quantitatively.
Suggested improvements are realistic but unprioritized. Discuss which change would yield the greatest precision gain and why.
Reference list exhibits inconsistent formatting (order of elements, missing DOIs). Adopt a standardized style (APA or MLA) and ensure completeness.

rubricData.name: Physics IA
rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â

similarExemplars.title:
To what extent does the varying of mass (0.010,0.020,0.030,0.040,0.050,0.060,0.070,0.080) kg
supported by a pulley system influence the maximum angular velocity of the wheels of a toy car rolling
up a slope (175cm), using video analysis software (Tracker)?

userSubmission.researchQuestion:
To what extent does the varying of mass (0.010,0.020,0.030,0.040,0.050,0.060,0.070,0.080) kg
supported by a pulley system influence the maximum angular velocity of the wheels of a toy car rolling
up a slope (175cm), using video analysis software (Tracker)?
